meta.title: Guardians of the Future — Pilot Proposal

hero.subtitle: A civic action initiative that helps communities turn care for place into visible environmental impact.

problem.eyebrow: The Problem - Why guardianship isn't scaling
problem.title:
Our communities and the places we depend on
need more guardians.
points: People care and want to help, but clear pathways to act together don't exist
Local efforts work in isolation, limiting what we can learn and achieve together
Without visibility and coordination, there's no collective momentum for real change
There's no standard way to measure and prove real environmental impact

approach.title: MVP Hypothesis
approach.description: The MVP explores how access to clear, actionable sustainability choices influences participation. When people can easily identify practical environmental actions, record their contributions, and see how those actions contribute to broader outcomes—like reduced waste or local environmental improvements—they understand how everyday behaviour connects to environmental progress.
approach.insight: This clarity strengthens personal agency and encourages people to participate alongside others pursuing similar goals. Over time, this shared visibility of action supports the development of an active and growing community.
approach.note: This guides the design and prioritisation of features that enable action, surface measurable outcomes, and support community interaction.

features.title: Circles
features.description: A Circle can be one person or a whole team. Each has its own identity, impact history, and profile. Everyone works through their Circle.

features.title: Challenge Library
features.description: Challenges with clear methods for impact. Adopt or create challenges and document the progress.

features.title: Impact Records
features.description: Every action gets timestamped and recorded. One data source. Everything pulls from the same record.

features.title: Recognition System
features.description: Markers appear based on actual contribution. No leaderboards, no competition — just honest acknowledgment.

cards.title: Joins
cards.body: She clicks a magic link, enters her name, city, and language. No password needed. She's in within two minutes.

cards.title: Adopts
cards.body: Her Circle browses the Challenge Library, reviews existing challenges, and picks the Neighbourhood Compost Sprint to tackle together.
cards.aside: Circles can adopt existing challenges or define their own.

cards.title: Reports
cards.body: 30 days later, they've composted 24kg. Amara submits a Level 2 report with a photo. An admin approves it within 48 hours.

cards.title: Impact
cards.body: The Impact Record gets its first entry: 24kg composted equals roughly 12kg CO₂e avoided. Seeing her action as a concrete number - not just a feeling - makes it real.
cards.aside: One shared data source. Everything pulls from the same place.

cards.title: Recognised
cards.body: Amara visits her profile and finds the First Impact marker waiting for her. She didn't have to ask, and her contribution is recognised.
cards.aside: Markers recognize what you've already done.

cards.title: Discovers
cards.body: She browses other Circles working on the same challenge. Some ahead, others starting. The collective impact is visible and growing - she's part of it.

tech.title: Built to grow, move fast, and earn trust

stack.technologies: Supabase · PostgreSQL · Row-Level Security · Real-time subscriptions
Single Impact Record table · Immutable data · Transparent methodology
Vercel · Automatic deployments · Edge functions · 99.9% uptime SLA

tech.note: Built for transparency. Every action tracked. All calculations visible. Nothing hidden.

phases.description: Live users · Real impact · Support & iteration

investment.title: The Numbers
investment.note: Payment tied to phase delivery · 50% deposit to commence · All amounts excl. VAT

closing.line: Every guardian starts somewhere. Let's start here.
closing.subtitle: Guardians of the Future · Pilot · March 2026
